title: Fast Responsive Left Panel Resize and Futuristic Enterprise UI Polish

edge_handle.location: middle-right edge of the map layer rail
edge_handle.accessible_label: Resize or collapse map layers panel
edge_handle.keyboard_support: Enter and Space toggle collapse or expand
edge_handle.old_header_collapse_button: removed from the Map Layers header

futuristic_styling_changes: Added a slim luminous glass edge handle for the layer rail.
Moved layer rail scrolling into an inner container so the outside handle is not clipped.
Added subtle enterprise panel depth, gradient border accents, and lower-cost glass styling.
Reduced global backdrop blur from 18px to 12px.
Replaced orb-like radial body accents with restrained linear dark executive gradients.
Improved layer card text wrapping so essential descriptions and legends are readable without hover.
Expanded hotspot concentration legend from cramped four-column chips into readable two-column rows.
Preserved the existing dark CFS product structure and map layer workflows.
performance_safeguards: No backend, PostGIS, data model, API, TopNav, parcel search, or SceneView data layer changes.
No heavy animations, video backgrounds, particles, or WebGL overlays added.
Resize uses pointer events and requestAnimationFrame with CSS variable updates instead of React state per pixel.
Grid-template-column transition removed so collapse/resize responds immediately.
Stored rail width is restored after mount to avoid SSR/client hydration mismatch.

validation_results.hydration_mismatch: fixed; no hydration mismatch found after final restart

smoke_test_results.frontend_loads: passed via dev:cfs and direct HTTP 200
smoke_test_results.left_panel_default_width: 360px by default, saved width restored after mount
smoke_test_results.edge_handle_present: verified in initial browser DOM check before browser navigation became unavailable
smoke_test_results.click_collapse_expand: implemented; initial browser check confirmed aria state changed, final visual retest was limited by in-app browser navigation timeouts
smoke_test_results.drag_resize: implemented with pointer events and requestAnimationFrame; final visual retest was limited by in-app browser navigation timeouts
smoke_test_results.fullscreen_behavior: source preserved existing right-panel and KPI hiding; edge handle remains part of layer rail
smoke_test_results.text_readability: layer labels and legends changed from truncated cramped rows to wrapping readable rows
smoke_test_results.selected_parcel_school_flood_fema_develo: API checks passed; feature code paths were not modified
smoke_test_results.browser_tool_limitation: The in-app browser CDP navigation timed out during final visual smoke testing, but the local app and APIs were healthy and logs showed no API failures.

remaining_ui_limitations: Manual visual confirmation in a normal browser is still recommended for the exact tactile feel of drag resizing.
ArcGIS label placement warnings may still appear for draped graphics; they are unrelated to this resize/UI polish pass.
Further premium polish could target individual cards, but this pass intentionally avoided broad layout redesign.
